_comment: Services that MUST NOT be disabled. Enforced by ps/apply/services.ps1 — will refuse without --iknowwhatimdoing regardless of what the plan JSON says. Two schemas accepted for backwards-compat: (a) legacy string value = reason only; (b) object with { reason, backs, addedAt }. New entries should use (b). 'backs' lists the UX flows / features that break if this service is disabled — critical for the 'hidden UX dependency' class of services (see incident 2026-07-07 in knowledge_base).

RpcSs.reason: Remote Procedure Call — the entire OS talks over this
backs: Every Windows service that talks to another

DcomLaunch.reason: DCOM Server Process Launcher — RPC dependency

PlugPlay.reason: Plug and Play — no devices come online without it

EventLog.reason: Windows Event Log — cascading failures if killed
backs: Every service that logs (all of them)

gpsvc.reason: Group Policy Client — login lockout if disabled
backs: policy application on any account (even standalone)

Themes.reason: Visual styles — logon hangs without it

ProfSvc.reason: User Profile Service — profile load failure = no login

CryptSvc.reason: Cryptographic Services — WU + code signing + TLS

BFE.reason: Base Filtering Engine — required by Windows Firewall
backs: WFP filters (many AV products use it)

MpsSvc.reason: Windows Firewall — do not disable, add rules instead

LanmanWorkstation.reason: SMB client — needed to access shares OR your own machine's admin shares

Dhcp.reason: DHCP client — you'll lose your IP
backs: Automatic IP assignment on any DHCP network (~everyone)

Dnscache.reason: DNS client cache — DNS still works but Chrome/Edge misbehave
backs: Chrome/Edge require it running (they refuse to start otherwise)

NlaSvc.reason: Network Location Awareness — firewall profile selection

AudioSrv.reason: Windows Audio — mute everything if disabled

AudioEndpointBuilder.reason: Audio endpoint enumeration — Windows Audio dependency

Schedule.reason: Task Scheduler — your automation dies without it

TrustedInstaller.reason: Windows Modules Installer — WU + optional features

wuauserv.reason: Windows Update — do not disable, use policy to control instead

sppsvc.reason: Software Protection — activation. Shows Auto/Stopped normally; that's correct.

backs: Chromium password prompts on some flows

KeyIso.reason: CNG Key Isolation — keys used by BitLocker etc

CDPSvc.reason: Connected Devices Platform Service — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Windows 11 Bluetooth 'Add device' wizard (silently fails to find anything without it)
CDPSvc.incident: Was disabled by services module Q11 (Copilot/UWP) inference. Broke BT pairing wizard on the seed machine.

fdPHost.reason: Function Discovery Provider Host — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Windows 11 Bluetooth 'Add device' wizard (window closes on click without it — this is the main culprit for the 2026-07-07 BT pairing incident)
Any 'Add a device' flow in Settings
fdPHost.incident: Was in Q6 (Casting to TV) Controls list. Answering 'no to casting' silently killed the BT pairing wizard because the same service backs both.

FDResPub.reason: Function Discovery Resource Publication — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Outgoing device advertisement (this PC appears in others' 'Add device' lists)
FDResPub.incident: Was in services_disable_safe legacy_networking group — silently disabled every run. Moved to tripwire.

NcdAutoSetup.reason: Network Connected Devices Auto-Setup — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
NcdAutoSetup.incident: Was miscategorized under enterprise_mdm. It backs consumer device onboarding too.

SSDPSRV.reason: SSDP Discovery — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: UPnP media renderer discovery (Cast to Device menu in Movies & TV / Groove)
Some BT+WiFi combo device onboarding flows
SSDPSRV.incident: Was in Q6 Controls list AND services_disable_safe legacy_networking. Double-disabled.

upnphost.reason: UPnP Device Host — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: This PC responding as a UPnP endpoint (Xbox Media Player finds it, phone finds it as DLNA target)
upnphost.incident: Was in services_disable_safe legacy_networking. Blindly disabled every run.

WFDSConMgrSvc.reason: Wi-Fi Direct Services Connection Manager — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Wi-Fi Direct printer connection (many modern printers)
Some BT+WiFi handoff flows during pairing

PhoneSvc.reason: Phone Service — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Bluetooth hands-free profile (BT audio devices with mic — headsets, car kits)
PhoneSvc.incident: Was assumed-disable for non-cellular laptops. But BT hands-free needs it — every BT headset with a mic uses it.

DeviceAssociationService.reason: Device pairing UI backend — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07

MapsBroker.reason: Downloaded Maps Manager — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Photos app map view for geotagged photos
MapsBroker.incident: Was in services_disable_safe windows_features_off_by_default. Not asked about, silently disabled. Copilot/Weather users get broken location behavior with no clue why.

MessagingService.reason: Messaging Service — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Cross-device Skype legacy on some builds
MessagingService.incident: Was labeled 'SMS (Phone Link)' in services_disable_safe as if optional. Phone Link is a heavily-used feature and users don't map 'do you use Copilot' → SMS relay.

WpnService.reason: Windows Push Notifications System Service — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07

WSService.reason: Windows Store license — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07

LicenseManager.reason: Windows License Manager — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Windows Subsystem for Linux app-store install

AppXSvc.reason: Appx Deployment Service — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07

StateRepository.reason: State Repository Service — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Appx package state (which apps are installed for whom)

TokenBroker.reason: Web Account Manager token broker — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-07
backs: Any modern-auth prompt in a UWP context

StiSvc.reason: Windows Image Acquisition (WIA) — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-08
backs: iPhone / iPad USB detection in iTunes (iOS devices expose themselves as imaging devices; AMDS driver stack depends on WIA)
iPhone appearing under 'This PC' in Explorer for photo import
Scanner button events + scanner UI (its original documented purpose)
StiSvc.incident: Was in services_disable_safe printer_scanner category. Q1 asked 'do you print or scan' — user with no printer answered NO, WIA got disabled, iPhone silently stopped appearing in iTunes. Same class as 2026-07-07 BT incident: single-feature question killed a multi-feature service.

Bonjour Service.reason: Apple Bonjour (mDNS) — hidden UX dep, added 2026-07-08
backs: iTunes / Apple Music device discovery (iPhone sync, Home Sharing)
Any third-party app that uses mDNS on Windows (some dev tools, IoT setup apps)
Bonjour Service.incident: Was in network_risky_features.json as 'redundant since Win10 has native mDNS' — but native mDNS does NOT cover iTunes device sync (that path goes through Bonjour+AMDS). Disable broke iPhone visibility in iTunes on 2026-07-08. Note: only present if iTunes / Adobe CC is installed; Get-Service returns nothing on machines without Apple software, so the tripwire is a no-op there.

Apple Mobile Device Service.reason: Apple Mobile Device Service (AMDS) — third-party, added 2026-07-08
backs: Every iOS device connection on Windows (iPhone / iPad / iPod)
Xcode / third-party MDM tools that talk to iOS devices
Apple Mobile Device Service.incident: Belt-and-suspenders. AMDS was NOT touched by the 2026-07-08 incident (Bonjour + StiSvc were), but any future 'clean up Apple bloat' rule risks catching this too. If the user has an iPhone, AMDS must stay.
